filename: HCL Proposal for 3DEx hosting and managed services on AWS for Bridgestone Americas.pptx

decomposition.title: DS 3DExperience Hosting and Managed Services on AWS

requirements.text: Vendor must support the environment for Teamcenter 13.x.
Vendor must provide separate environments for VW: Development (8), Test & Integration (6), QS (2), Prod (1), plus China JV QS (2) and Prod (2).
Vendor solution must handle ~4,000-5,000 concurrent users.
Vendor solution must support up to 37,695 user-licenses with approximately 13,000-16,000 active users per month.
Vendor must migrate the VW-CONNECT application from on-premises to AWS Cloud Infrastructure.
Vendor must assess the on-prem VW-CONNECT application and infrastructure to define target deployment architecture on AWS.
Vendor must evaluate and provide a data migration strategy for Database and Vault/Volume data from on-premises to Cloud.
Vendor must design and provision AWS Infrastructure and migrate the VW-CONNECT application in all Dev, T&I, QS, and Prod environments sequentially.
Vendor must ensure infrastructure and application security and compliance are maintained throughout the engagement.
Vendor must handle large data volumes, including a database of ~10.5TB growing at ~215GB/month and vault data of ~1.2PB growing at ~9.66TB/month.
Vendor must host the solution in the AWS US East (N. Virginia) region.
Vendor must provide AWS Cloud Infrastructure provisioning as part of Infrastructure Management.
Vendor must provide OS image and patch management as part of Infrastructure Management.
Vendor must provide Database management as part of Infrastructure Management.
Vendor must provide Backup management as part of Infrastructure Management.
Vendor must provide AWS network implementation and support as part of Infrastructure Management.
Vendor must handle incident, request, change, and problem management for infrastructure components.
Vendor must perform 3DExperience installation and configuration.
Vendor must handle patching and Hot Fix management for 3DExperience.
Vendor must handle build, release, and deployment for the 3DExperience platform.
Vendor must handle incident, request, change, and problem management for the 3DExperience platform.
Vendor must provide Security services including IAM, AD/LDAP/SSO Integration, Data Security, and Encryption.
Vendor must provide logging and monitoring for infrastructure (CPU, Memory, Networking) and platform, including alerts and notifications.
Vendor must provide risk and compliance support, including audit support, risk remediation, and adherence to required regulations.

Vendor must staff the roles for managed services, including AWS Infra Architect, AWS Infra Admin, 3DExperience Deployment Architect, 3DExperience Deployment Admin, Database Administrator, and Project Manager.

Vendor must provide PLM services including PLM implementation, installation, configuration, customization, cloud transformation, data migration, enterprise application integration, and PLM incident management (L1/L2/L3).
Vendor must handle software license management for PLM software, OS, and DB if required.
Vendor must handle infrastructure services, including provisioning (VM, Storage, Network, DB), maintenance, patching, monitoring, and infra incident management (L1/L2/L3).
Vendor must manage the Cloud Infra subscription, including infrastructure billing and payment, as needed.
All communication and documentation must be in English.
Vendor can optionally provide an onsite 3DEXP ADMIN at Akron, Ohio, at an additional cost.
requirements.page_ref: [AWS Infra Services & Platform Services Ballpark Pricing]

ambiguities: The document references both Teamcenter 13.x (Siemens) and 3DExperience (Dassault) in different sections, creating uncertainty about the actual PLM platform in scope.

similar_deals.title: SAP S/4HANA Global Rollout
similar_deals.why_relevant: Large automotive client in Germany with global scope and significant transformation demands, providing partial parallels in scale and complexity.
similar_deals.key_lesson: Executive sponsorship and prior references in the automotive domain (BMW) were decisive in winning large-scale global rollouts.

win_intel.win_probability_rationale: We have large-scale automotive references (e.g., BMW) and experience delivering global rollouts, but no direct Teamcenter 13.x or VW environment references. Concurrency and user-license scale are untested in our documented history. Without confirming competitor data, we maintain moderate caution. Our domain references help, yet unproven Teamcenter credentials lower confidence, leading to an estimated 35% win probability.
capability_gaps: No documented reference for Teamcenter 13.x migrations
Unproven track record in handling ~4,000–5,000 concurrent users
Lack of VW-specific environment management references
recommended_win_themes: Leverage automotive portfolio references (e.g., BMW) to underscore industry expertise
Highlight methodology and tooling for large on-prem to cloud migrations
Showcase robust security, compliance, and data handling for high-volume PLM environments
Demonstrate executive-level sponsorship and rapid mobilization capabilities
risk_factors: Uncertainty in Teamcenter 13.x experience could erode client confidence
Complex concurrency demands and data volumes pose scalability risks
Multi-region environment, including China JV, introduces regulatory and operational complexities
must_fix_before_bidding: Establish credible references or partnerships for Teamcenter 13.x expertise
Document proven solution architecture for high concurrency and large data volumes
Confirm readiness for multi-environment separation (Dev, T&I, QS, Prod) aligned with VW standards

client_intel.recommended_narrative: Because no public information is available, we cannot confirm any specific priorities or initiatives. Our best approach is to emphasize flexibility and scalability with DS 3DExperience Hosting and Managed Services on AWS, highlighting general benefits such as cost efficiency, modernization potential, and operational resilience.

competitors.predicted_positioning: Likely to emphasize large-scale cloud migration expertise, referencing success with US Federal contracts such as the $73M PRELIMINARY DESIGN REVIEW INC. 1 (public contract). Confidence: medium.
competitors.predicted_price_range_usd: Likely around $900K–$1.2M, smaller scale than their typical $9.7M–$73M federal awards. Financial data unavailable. Confidence: low.
their_strengths: Global delivery network and extensive US presence (public US Federal contracts).
Broad cloud transformation credentials from large-scale government projects.
their_weaknesses: No publicly cited references with DS 3DExperience or Teamcenter specifically.
No clear evidence of Germany presence or recent contracts in that region.
how_to_beat_them: Show proven Teamcenter 13.x and 3DExperience success stories in automotive.
Stress local Germany coverage and specialized AWS cloud migration frameworks.

competitors.predicted_positioning: Will emphasize end-to-end digital transformation and global scale, citing massive US Federal wins (e.g., $1.5B TIVOD support). Confidence: medium.
competitors.predicted_price_range_usd: Likely around $1M–$1.3M given the project scope is significantly smaller than their typical 9-figure contracts. Financial data unavailable. Confidence: low.
their_strengths: Huge global footprint and brand recognition in cloud and enterprise systems.
Experience executing major US Federal contracts at scale (public awards).
their_weaknesses: Limited public proof of Teamcenter 13.x or DS 3DExperience references.
No job postings suggesting a targeted push for this specific automotive RFP.
how_to_beat_them: Highlight niche expertise for automotive PLM migrations and data volumes.
Underscore a faster, more cost-effective approach than Accenture’s likely premium solution.

competitors.predicted_positioning: May highlight application modernization and data migration expertise, referencing smaller US Federal data optimization contracts (e.g., $2.4M DB2 optimization). Confidence: medium.
competitors.predicted_price_range_usd: Likely around $850K–$1.15M, referencing prior mid-range federal deals. Financial data unavailable. Confidence: low.
their_strengths: Strong offshore cost structure and track record in database migration (citing $2.4M DB2 optimization).
Broad cloud implementation capabilities across multiple industries.
their_weaknesses: No publicly known Teamcenter 13.x or DS 3DExperience credentials.
No recent contract wins in Germany or explicit job postings linked to this domain.
how_to_beat_them: Emphasize specialized PLM domain credentials and direct automotive client references.
Leverage deeper local partner ecosystem in Germany for compliance and support.

competitors.predicted_positioning: Likely to focus on enterprise cloud migration success, citing US Federal ‘Enterprise Cloud Common Shared Services’ deal ($63M). Confidence: medium.
competitors.predicted_price_range_usd: Likely around $900K–$1.2M, smaller scope than their $39M–$63M public federal contracts. Financial data unavailable. Confidence: low.
their_strengths: Notable track record in large-scale cloud transitions (source: $63M enterprise cloud contract).
Global consulting reach and cross-industry credentials.
their_weaknesses: Lack of public references specifically related to Teamcenter or DS 3DExperience.
No explicit hiring signals or Germany-specific contract wins for this domain.
how_to_beat_them: Stress proven capability in automotive PLM transformations and large data volumes.
Highlight specialized methods for rapid, secure AWS migrations in multiple global locations.

recommended_differentiators: Demonstrate explicit success migrating Teamcenter 13.x in the automotive sector to AWS.
Show proven approach to handling large TB- to PB-scale data migration securely.
Highlight local Germany and US compliance expertise to meet Bridgestone Americas’ global needs.
competitor.killer_differentiator: We have proprietary VW-CONNECT migration accelerators, specifically engineered for Teamcenter 13.x and DS 3DExperience data volumes, enabling a rapid, risk-mitigated transition on AWS—no other competitor fields proven automotive PLM migration frameworks at this scale.

ghost_bids.likely_solution_approach: TCS would likely rely on its large-scale cloud migration credentials from prior US Federal projects, applying a similar methodology of phased migration and global offshore delivery. They would propose using standard discovery and data migration frameworks but have limited direct experience with DS 3DExperience and Teamcenter 13.x.
ghost_bids.predicted_pricing_range_usd: Insufficient financial data for margin-based pricing. Based on prior mid-range deals (e.g., $73M US Federal project), TCS would likely propose around $900K–$1.2M for this 3-year scope.
predicted_win_themes: Global delivery model and cost-effective offshore resources
Proven success in complex US Federal cloud projects
ghost_bids.predicted_team_model: Primarily offshore-led with limited on-site resources in the US, leveraging global delivery to keep costs competitive.
key_vulnerabilities: No documented references for DS 3DExperience or Teamcenter 13.x
Reliance on generic cloud migration methodologies rather than automotive-centric techniques
how_we_beat_this_bid: Emphasize proven automotive PLM migration references involving Teamcenter 13.x
Highlight local presence in Germany and specific compliance/region knowledge
Showcase migration accelerators custom-built for large data volumes and DS 3DExperience integration

ghost_bids.likely_solution_approach: Accenture would present an end-to-end digital transformation strategy, leveraging global scale and brand recognition. They might propose a robust governance and program management layer, referencing their large US Federal wins but with less apparent specialization in Teamcenter 13.x or DS 3DExperience.
ghost_bids.predicted_pricing_range_usd: Insufficient SEC financial data for an exact pricing floor. Based on similar smaller-scale wins and brand premium, they likely price around $1M–$1.3M total for 3 years.
predicted_win_themes: Global brand recognition and operational scale
Comprehensive transformation approach combining strategy, process, and technology
Established cloud migration frameworks from major federal contracts
ghost_bids.predicted_team_model: Extensive onshore leadership and architecture teams combined with offshore technical resources, structured under an Accenture global delivery approach.
key_vulnerabilities: No explicit public references for Teamcenter 13.x or DS 3DExperience
Potentially higher overall project costs due to premium brand and overhead
No recent Germany-specific contract wins or local job postings to indicate strong local presence
how_we_beat_this_bid: Show niche PLM domain knowledge and proven success with Teamcenter 13.x migrations
Demonstrate a faster, more budget-friendly approach by leveraging proven automotive frameworks
Highlight local collaboration capabilities to ensure compliance in Germany and the United States

ghost_bids.likely_solution_approach: Infosys would emphasize its application modernization and database migration experience from prior US Federal data deals (e.g., $2.4M DB2 optimization), focusing on migrating on-premise data to AWS. They would not have specialized DS 3DExperience or Teamcenter references to highlight.
ghost_bids.predicted_pricing_range_usd: Insufficient financial data for a precise margin-based calculation. Based on smaller federal deals, they are likely to price around $850K–$1.15M for this 3-year engagement.
predicted_win_themes: Broad cloud transformation capabilities across multiple industries
ghost_bids.predicted_team_model: A lean onshore team for project management and client interfacing, with the main technical execution delivered offshore to optimize costs.
key_vulnerabilities: No public references for DS 3DExperience or Teamcenter 13.x
Lack of recent Germany contract wins or explicit hiring efforts in the region
how_we_beat_this_bid: Leverage direct automotive client references and proven Teamcenter 13.x success stories
Demonstrate a robust local presence or partnerships in Germany for compliance assurance
Show specialized data migration accelerators tuned for large PLM data volumes

ghost_bids.likely_solution_approach: Capgemini would likely present an enterprise-scale cloud migration approach referencing their federal ‘Enterprise Cloud Common Shared Services’ contract ($63M). They would stress global consulting capabilities, but with limited DS 3DExperience or Teamcenter credentials.
ghost_bids.predicted_pricing_range_usd: Insufficient financial data for a margin-based floor. Using prior mid-range federal contract data, likely $900K–$1.2M for the 3-year term.
predicted_win_themes: Global consulting footprint and cross-industry knowledge
Methodical project management frameworks proven in federal engagements
ghost_bids.predicted_team_model: Matrixed onshore-offshore model, with consulting leads in the US or Europe and delivery teams offshore for day-to-day support.
key_vulnerabilities: No proven track record specifically with DS 3DExperience or Teamcenter 13.x
No explicit Germany contract wins or localized automotive references
Generic cloud migration approach may lack specialized PLM expertise
how_we_beat_this_bid: Highlight proven automotive PLM transformations and dedicated accelerators for DS 3DExperience
Focus on robust data migration strategies designed specifically for large PLM databases

ghost_bids.overall_competitive_position: All four competitors show medium likelihood to bid but lack direct Teamcenter 13.x or DS 3DExperience credentials. Their generic cloud migration offerings create an opening for our specialized PLM expertise to stand out.
ghost_bids.single_biggest_risk: The main risk is that a big brand competitor might leverage their global scale and deep discounting to overshadow our specialized credentials if the client focuses solely on cost or brand reputation.
ghost_bids.recommended_counter_strategy: Reinforce our proven automotive PLM migration accelerators, emphasize local Germany support, highlight references for DS 3DExperience and Teamcenter 13.x, and underscore our ability to handle massive data volumes securely on AWS.

deal_fingerprint.deal_archetype: Global Automotive PLM Cloud Migration & Managed Services
similar_public_contracts: CLOUD PRODUCTS&TOOLS (CPT) | Winner: GENERAL DYNAMICS INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY, INC. | $902,031,452
MDA INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (IT) SERVICES | Winner: NORTHROP GRUMMAN SYSTEMS CORPORATION | $579,217,436
deal_fingerprint.historical_win_rate_for_archetype: Around 30% (relatively low success rate unless fully aligned with PLM & AWS expertise)
critical_success_factors: Robust AWS cloud design and referenceable experience
Clear data migration strategy for large datasets
common_failure_modes: Unclear scope regarding mixed PLM platforms (Dassault vs Siemens)
Underestimating data migration complexity and performance requirements
Insufficient cost modeling for high AWS usage
Failure to address large concurrency and global location constraints
bid_conditions: Validate concurrency requirements for price alignment
Obtain commitment for bridging the on-prem to AWS migration complexities
Ensure budget covers AWS infra, performance, and data transfer

solution_options.name: Premium / Lowest Risk / Highest Quality
solution_options.description: Multi-region AWS deployment with full HA/DR, robust security controls, and highest capacity. Designed for maximum performance and lowest downtime risk.
solution_options.rationale: This option provides the most robust architecture (multi-region failover, maximum capacity overhead) and thus carries the highest infrastructure and support costs.

solution_options.name: Balanced / Recommended
solution_options.description: Single-region AWS deployment with HA in one primary region, cost-optimized storage classes, and a moderate support staffing approach.
solution_options.rationale: Balanced architecture meets performance needs at lower cost, leveraging reserved instances and standard AWS redundancy while controlling overhead.

solution_options.name: Lean / Lowest Cost / Higher Risk
solution_options.description: Minimal redundancy single-region approach using cost-saving measures (spot instances, lower SLA), streamlined support team, and more aggressive resource sharing.
key_components: Spot instances and cheaper storage tiers
Higher risk of downtime under peak load
solution_options.rationale: Lowest possible cost for hosting and services, but with risk of outages, slower recovery times, and constraints that may impact performance.

pricing.competitive_rationale: Competitor’s total of $850k-$1.2M appears to exclude realistic cloud infrastructure costs for this scale of data and concurrency. Our proposed pricing reflects actual AWS costs plus reasonable margins to ensure performance, security, and compliance requirements.

draft.rfp_id: DS 3DExperience Hosting and Managed Services on AWS
draft.executive_summary: Bridgestone Americas (BSA) seeks a flexible, scalable DS 3DExperience hosting and managed services solution on AWS, yet no public details of the CTO’s priorities are available. HCLTech’s approach thus centers on adaptability, cost efficiency, and robust modernization—ensuring operational resilience and performance. Our proven automotive portfolio, including successes with BMW, positions us to deliver an effective, industry-aligned migration of DS 3DExperience. By leveraging our proprietary VW-CONNECT accelerators, we will reduce risk and time-to-value, enabling BSA to bolster its competitive edge while focusing on driving innovation in Germany, the United States, and beyond.
win_themes: Automotive portfolio references (BMW) emphasizing industry expertise
Methodology and tooling for large on-prem to cloud migrations
Robust security, compliance, and data handling for high-volume PLM environments
Executive-level sponsorship and rapid mobilization capabilities
Proprietary VW-CONNECT migration accelerators for Teamcenter 13.x and DS 3DExperience data volumes

sections.content: Bridgestone Americas operates within a dynamic automotive sector, requiring extensive product lifecycle management (PLM) capabilities to maintain a competitive edge. Legacy on-premises DS 3DExperience infrastructure can constrain modernization initiatives, limit global collaboration, and inflate operating expenses. The complexity of scaling across multiple geographies—particularly Germany and the United States—amplifies regulatory considerations and demands advanced security measures. By shifting DS 3DExperience to AWS, BSA can unlock improved agility, drive operational efficiencies, and comply with stringent industry and data protection standards.

HCLTech proposes a balanced, single-region AWS deployment with high availability (HA) in the primary region. This configuration optimizes costs through reserved instances and tiered storage while ensuring reliability. Our proprietary VW-CONNECT migration accelerators, custom-built for DS 3DExperience and Teamcenter 13.x automotive data sets, expedite and derisk the migration process. Core security features, including encryption, multi-factor authentication, and compliance alignment with ISO 27001 and TISAX, safeguard confidential design information. We deliver a moderate, yet efficient, support model with business-hours coverage and on-call emergency response. Over 36 months, our approach accelerates ROI while preserving solution stability and regulatory compliance.

Our delivery framework begins with a comprehensive assessment of existing PLM workloads, infrastructure, and compliance requirements. We then establish a phased migration plan that uses incremental data loads and rigorous testing to ensure minimal downtime. Throughout, we employ agile techniques for iterative feedback and continuous improvement, supported by established project management methodologies. HCLTech’s dedicated lead architects oversee the technical execution, coordinating closely with Bridgestone stakeholders. This approach fosters transparent collaboration and mitigates risk, propelling a swift, secure cutover to AWS.

HCLTech’s engagement team features AWS-certified engineers, DS 3DExperience specialists, and project managers with extensive automotive experience. We have delivered complex PLM transitions for global brands like BMW, resulting in deep process knowledge and lessons learned. Our automotive leads understand the nuance of compliance, data volume, and performance requirements unique to the industry. Executive sponsorship ensures swift mobilization and strategic alignment, empowering the team to achieve early wins and customer satisfaction.

HCLTech stands apart due to our automotive domain expertise, proven migration accelerators, and commitment to security excellence. Our proprietary VW-CONNECT solution uniquely addresses DS 3DExperience and Teamcenter 13.x challenges, enabling a faster, more reliable cutover to AWS. With global delivery centers across Germany, the United States, and beyond, we support enterprises wherever they operate. By combining best-practice governance with executive-level sponsorship, we deliver outcomes aligned with your strategic priorities. We look forward to partnering with Bridgestone for a sustainable, innovation-driven future.

draft.architecture_diagram:
graph TD
    subgraph Single-Region AWS Deployment
    DSMain[DS 3DExperience Primary Node]
    DSStandby[DS 3DExperience Secondary Node]
    RDS[(AWS RDS)]
    S3[(AWS S3 Storage with Lifecycle Policies)]
    DSMain --> RDS
    DSStandby --> RDS
    DSMain --> S3
    DSStandby --> S3
    end
    SupportDesk[Business-Hours + On-Call Support]
    Single-Region AWS Deployment --> SupportDesk

critical_issues: Claim of large-scale BMW references is unsupported by the data
Claim of proprietary VW-CONNECT migration accelerators for Teamcenter 13.x is unsupported
All competitor pricing and deal-size claims lack any matching federal data
Recommended price ($8.1M) is not corroborated by any sourcing data
removed_claims: All references to BMW or automotive domain experience
All TCS / Accenture / Infosys / Capgemini price range statements
warnings: No data found in the provided sources to confirm any competitor or automotive references
All pricing and capability claims remain unverified
